overview: Re-joins multiple TTX documents (previously split by the **TTX Splitter Step**) back into single documents. The step takes raw documents as input and outputs the resulting joined files in the same directory as the input documents. Input files are automatically sorted by their part markers before joining. Files without valid part markers are ignored with a warning.

suffix.help:
A marker text appended to the end of the output filename (before the `.ttx` extension area). For example, if the original file was `index.html` and the suffix is `_CONCAT`, the output will be `index.html_CONCAT.ttx`.

If a file with the resulting name already exists in the output directory, it will be **overwritten without warning**.
notes: **Warning:** Existing files with the same output name will be overridden silently — no confirmation or backup is created.
examples: Suffix `_CONCAT` with input parts `index.html_part001.ttx` and `index.html_part002.ttx` → output `index.html_CONCAT.ttx`

limitations: This step is triggered at the `END_BATCH` event, meaning it runs only after all documents in the batch have been processed.
Input files with no part marker or an invalid part marker are silently ignored (with a warning logged).
processingNotes: Input files are automatically sorted internally so that parts are joined in the correct order, regardless of the order they appear in the input file list.
The received event is passed through unaltered — the joined output file is written as a side effect to the same directory as the input documents.

examples.title: Joining split TTX parts with a custom suffix
examples.description: Four TTX part files from two original documents are joined back together. The step sorts them by part number and produces one output file per original document.
